subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Main range** — Rulebook supports **2-7 players**.
- **Psychic token scaling** — "Each psychic receives" clairvoyancy tokens by count:
  - **2-3 players** — 0 clairvoyancy tokens.
  - **4-5 players** — 4 clairvoyancy tokens (numbered 1-4).
  - **6-7 players** — 6 clairvoyancy tokens (numbered 1-6).
- **Ghost setup scaling** — Rulebook has a dedicated setup diagram for **4-7 player games** with separate **4-5** and **6-7** placements.
- **Low-count variant** — Rulebook has a full **Two- and Three-Player Game** variant with adjusted setup and final-phase rules.
- **Difficulty table scaling** — Number of character/location/object cards laid out varies by **players (3-7)** and **difficulty (Easy/Medium/Hard)**.

#### Psychic Components
- **Intuition tokens** — 6 total (1 per color).
- **Sleeves** — 6 total (1 per color).
- **Clairvoyancy level markers** — 6 total (1 per color).
- **Clairvoyancy tokens** — 36 total (6 per color).
- **Clock board** — 1.
- **Progress boards** — 4 total: character, location, object, epilogue.
- **Psychic cards** — 54 total:
  - 18 character cards (rear 1-18)
  - 18 location cards (rear 19-36)
  - 18 object cards (rear 37-54)

#### Ghost Components
- **Game screen** — 1.
- **Sand timer** — 1.
- **Ghost cards** — 54 total:
  - 18 character ghost cards
  - 18 location ghost cards
  - 18 object ghost cards
- **Vision cards** — 84.
- **Ghost tokens** — 6 (1 per color).
- **Culprit tokens** — 6 (rear 1-6).
- **Clairvoyancy track** — 1 board element.

#### Player-Count Rule Blocks
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy setup** — No clairvoyancy track markers/tokens used for standard voting system.
- **Variant availability** — Special "Two- and Three-Player Game" rules apply.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tokens per psychic** — 4 (numbered 1-4).

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tokens per psychic** — 6 (numbered 1-6).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Layout Sequence
1. **Place clock board** — Set hour hand to I (1h).
2. **Place progress boards** — Character, Location, Object, and Epilogue.
3. **Place psychic card rows** — Character, location, and object options per setup table.
4. **Place ghost screen** — Ghost keeps hidden assignments behind screen.
5. **Place vision deck** — Behind screen; ghost draws 7-card hand.
6. **Place clairvoyancy track area** — If using standard 4-7 player clairvoyancy system.

#### Ghost Screen Organization
- **Columns** — One column per psychic.
- **Rows** — Character row, location row, object row.
- **Ghost tokens** — Place color ghost token at bottom of each column as turn reminder.

#### Difficulty + Player Count Card Layout (from table)
- **What scales** — Number of character/location/object cards shown in each category.
- **Axes** — Difficulty (Easy/Medium/Hard) × player count (3-7 shown in table excerpt).
- **Practical check** — Set all three categories (character/location/object) using the same table rule for your chosen difficulty and player count.

#### Difficulty × Player-Count Card Layout

--- 3 Players (2 Psychics) ---
- **Easy** — Lay out **4** cards per category. **Medium** — **5** cards. **Hard** — **6** cards.

--- 4 Players (3 Psychics) ---
- **Easy** — **5** cards per category. **Medium** — **6** cards. **Hard** — **7** cards.

--- 5 Players (4 Psychics) ---
- **Easy** — **5** cards per category. **Medium** — **6** cards. **Hard** — **7** cards.

--- 6 Players (5 Psychics) ---
- **Easy** — **6** cards per category. **Medium** — **7** cards. **Hard** — **8** cards.

--- 7 Players (6 Psychics) ---
- **Easy** — **6** cards per category. **Medium** — **7** cards. **Hard** — **8-9** cards.

#### Player-Count Layout Notes
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Use variant rules** — Two-and-three-player variant changes final-phase setup.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Use standard multiplayer layout** — Follow 4-5 placement references shown in setup diagrams.

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Use standard multiplayer layout** — Follow 6-7 placement references shown in setup diagrams.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Role Assignment
- **One ghost** — Exactly one player takes the ghost role.
- **All others psychics** — Remaining players are psychics.

#### Psychic Setup
- **Take color set** — Intuition token, sleeve, clairvoyancy level marker.
- **Set clairvoyancy marker** — Start at 0 on clairvoyancy track (standard mode).
- **Receive clairvoyancy tokens** — Based on player-count block below.

#### Ghost Setup
- **Prepare hidden combinations** — One character + one location + one object per psychic.
- **Draw opening hand** — 7 vision cards.
- **Keep culprit tokens** — Behind screen for final phase.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tokens received** — 0.
- **Special mode option** — Use two-and-three-player variant if desired.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tokens received** — 4 per psychic (numbered 1-4).

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tokens received** — 6 per psychic (numbered 1-6).

#### Two-and-Three-Player Variant Setup (explicit rulebook block)
- **Clairvoyancy system removed** — In **2-3 player variant games**, do not use clairvoyancy track, related markers, or clairvoyancy tokens.
- **Psychic control** — In **2-3 player variant games**, each player controls two psychics.
- **Shared vision visibility** — In **2-3 player variant games**, all three shared-vision cards are face up.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start-of-Game Conditions
1. **Set turn clock** — Begin at hour I.
2. **Place intuition tokens** — Psychics start on Character progress board.
3. **Ghost hand ready** — Ghost has 7 vision cards.
4. **Begin Phase 1** — Reconstruction of events starts.

#### Investigation Order (fixed)
- **Step order** — Character first, then Location, then Object.
- **No skipping** — Psychics cannot jump to later category before solving current one.

#### Time Limit
- **Maximum turns** — 7 hours (I through VII).
- **Failure threshold** — If not all psychics finish by end of VII, game is lost.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Variant start state** — Two psychics per player, open shared-vision handling in final phase.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Standard start state** — Clairvoyancy system active.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Phase 1 Turn Sequence (Reconstruction of Events)
1. **Ghost distributes visions** — At least one vision card to each psychic.
2. **Psychics interpret** — Discuss and place intuition tokens on current-category cards.
3. **Timer window closes** — Sand timer ends discussion.
4. **Ghost manifests results** — Confirms each psychic as correct/incorrect.
5. **Advance progress** — Move solved psychics forward; unresolved psychics remain.
6. **Advance clock** — Move hour hand one step.
7. **Check state** — Continue Phase 1, move to Phase 2, or lose at VII if unresolved.

#### Phase 2 Sequence (Revealing the Culprit)
1. **Suspect line-up** — Build grouped combinations and number them.
2. **Shared vision** — Ghost provides 3-card final clue and sets culprit token secretly.
3. **Straw poll** — Psychics vote for culprit group; reveal and resolve.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Variant procedure** — Uses special open-vote/open-shared-vision adjustments.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Standard procedure** — Secret votes with clairvoyancy-informed reveal timing.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Ghost Actions (Phase 1)
- **Assign vision cards** — Give one or more cards to a psychic.
- **Mark completion** — Push that psychic’s color ghost token to indicate they were served this turn.
- **Refill hand** — Draw back to 7 after assigning visions.
- **Repeat** — Continue until all psychics have at least one new card this turn.

#### Ghost Discard/Redraw Limits (Difficulty)
- **Easy** — Up to once per turn.
- **Medium** — Up to three times per game.
- **Hard** — Once per game.

#### Psychic Actions (Phase 1)
- **Interpret clues** — Compare current visions against category row.
- **Place intuition token** — Choose a target card in current category.
- **Reposition before timer end** — Move token freely until timer expires.
- **Retain clues on failure** — Keep old visions if wrong; receive additional cards next turn.

#### Clairvoyancy Actions (standard mode)
- **Place agree/disagree token** — On other psychics’ intuition picks only.
- **One per target token** — Do not stack multiple clairvoyancy tokens on same target intuition token from same player.
- **Multiple placements allowed** — You may bet on several psychics in one turn.
- **Discard after use** — Used clairvoyancy tokens go to discard area and return at specified timing.

#### Resolution Actions
- **If correct** — Psychic sleeves solved card, discards visions, and advances to next progress board.
- **If incorrect** — Psychic stays on same board and keeps visions for additive future clues.
- **If fully completed (3 solved)** — Move intuition token to mission accomplished and gain remaining-hour clairvoyancy advancement.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Variant voting system** — No clairvoyancy token betting.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Standard clairvoyancy scale** — 4 numbered clairvoyancy tokens per psychic.

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Standard clairvoyancy scale** — 6 numbered clairvoyancy tokens per psychic.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Core Asymmetry
- **Ghost role** — Knows hidden assignments and communicates only via vision cards.
- **Psychic role** — Interprets images and advances through fixed category order.

#### Clairvoyancy Track System
- **Purpose** — Rewards accurate reads of other psychics.
- **Final-phase impact** — Higher clairvoyancy reveals more shared-vision information before voting.

#### Shared Vision Mechanic (Phase 2)
- **Card count** — 3 vision cards form the shared vision.
- **Intended mapping** — One card should point to character, one to location, one to object in same suspect group.
- **Culprit lock** — Ghost places matching culprit token face down in culprit space.

#### Tie-Break Mechanics in Final Vote
- **Primary tie-break** — Group selected by psychic furthest on clairvoyancy track.
- **Secondary tie-break** — If still tied, eldest tied player’s group.

#### Player-Count Variant Rules
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **System removed** — No clairvoyancy track/markers/tokens.
- **Dual-psychic control** — Each player controls two psychics.
- **Shared vision visibility** — Three shared-vision cards are face up.
- **Suspect line-up adjustment** — Add two random extra groups from discarded psychic cards to reach four groups total.
- **Voting style** — Vote openly, not secretly.
- **Final vote marker use** — Player controlling two psychics uses one intuition token to designate suspected culprit group.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Standard mode** — Secret straw poll with clairvoyancy-tiered information reveal.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End States
- **No VP scoring race** — Mysterium is cooperative win/loss.
- **Team result** — Everyone wins together or loses together.

#### Loss Condition A (Phase 1 timeout)
- **Clock limit** — If any psychic has not completed character+location+object by end of hour VII, all players lose.

#### Win/Loss Condition B (Phase 2 vote)
- **Win** — Majority-selected suspect group matches hidden culprit token.
- **Loss** — Majority-selected suspect group does not match culprit token.

#### Final Vote Resolution Order
1. **Reveal votes** — Place votes onto numbered suspect groups.
2. **Find majority** — Highest vote group is team accusation.
3. **Resolve ties** — Use clairvoyancy position, then eldest player tie-break.
4. **Reveal culprit token** — Compare accusation to true culprit.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Final vote style** — Open voting.
- **Line-up size** — Four suspect groups after adding two random extra groups.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Final vote style** — Secret voting with clairvoyancy-based information timing.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Build a clue language early** — Agree how your table reads color, mood, objects, and symbolism.
- **Ghost clarity first** — Use high-separation clues in first hours to prevent early stalls.
- **Narrow before deep debate** — Eliminate obvious non-matches quickly, then compare top candidates.
- **Track the clock constantly** — Hour economy matters more than perfect certainty.
- **Support the slowest lane** — A single unresolved psychic can lose the game at VII.

#### Player-Count Focus
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Cognitive load** — Each player runs two psychics; keep discussions structured by "Psychic A then Psychic B" to avoid cross-confusion.
- **Open shared vision benefit** — Use face-up shared vision to align quickly in final phase.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Clairvoyancy tempo** — Use your 4 clairvoyancy tokens proactively; do not hoard all bets for late game.

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Table discipline** — More voices means more noise; assign one player to summarize consensus before timer expires.
- **Token leverage** — 6 clairvoyancy tokens allow broader betting; spread bets where confidence is highest.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Ghost clues that are too broad
- **Why it fails** — Psychics split across too many plausible cards.
- **Fix** — Use image sets that push one primary interpretation.

#### Mistake 2 — Literal-only reading by psychics
- **Why it fails** — Many correct clues are thematic or emotional, not object-exact.
- **Fix** — Evaluate color, tone, composition, and story cues.

#### Mistake 3 — Ignoring order lock
- **Why it fails** — Discussing later categories before current solve wastes timer.
- **Fix** — Keep each psychic focused on their current progress board.

#### Mistake 4 — Clairvoyancy hoarding (standard mode)
- **Why it fails** — Late unused tokens create lost advancement opportunities.
- **Fix** — Place informed agree/disagree bets early and often.

#### Mistake 5 — Solved players disengaging
- **Why it fails** — Team loses high-value external analysis support.
- **Fix** — Solved players pivot to helper role immediately.

#### Mistake 6 — Final-vote groupthink
- **Why it fails** — Confident wrong anchor can pull whole table.
- **Fix** — Require each player to state evidence before committing consensus.

#### Player-Count Notes
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Variant risk** — Managing two psychics per player increases cognitive errors; use turn-by-turn verbal checklists.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Variant risk** — Secret voting can hide misalignment until reveal; clarify assumptions during shared-vision discussion.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Precision vs flexibility in ghost clueing
- **Precision approach** — Faster solves when read correctly.
- **Flexible approach** — Safer when candidates are visually similar but may cost extra turns.

#### Decision 2 — Commit early vs wait for timer-end data
- **Early commit** — Leaves room for teammates to react with clairvoyancy bets.
- **Late commit** — Uses more discussion input but risks rushed mistakes.

#### Decision 3 — Bet concentrated vs bet distributed (standard mode)
- **Concentrated betting** — Bigger confidence play on one likely-correct psychic.
- **Distributed betting** — Lower variance across multiple likely outcomes.

#### Decision 4 — Rescue lagging psychic vs optimize leading psychics
- **Rescue line** — Best when clock is tight and one player is stuck.
- **Optimize line** — Best when finishing one more psychic unlocks better final information timing.

#### Player-Count Sections
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Split attention between two controlled psychics without mixing clue contexts.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Spend limited 4-token clairvoyancy resources where expected value is highest.

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Key trade-off** — Balance broad token deployment with table-noise control so final votes remain coherent.

metadata.notes: Written from provided Mysterium rulebook text excerpts as primary source.
